diagrams.title: Basic Aggregation View Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the fundamental structure of an aggregation view, displaying how base tables (e.g., Sales table with transaction data) feed into an aggregation view that computes summary values (e.g., total sales, average sales) using aggregate functions like SUM, AVG, COUNT.
elements: Arrow showing data flow from table to view
diagrams.purpose: Explains the core concept of what aggregation views are and how they transform raw data into summarized information

diagrams.title: GROUP BY Aggregation in Views
diagrams.description: Illustrates how aggregation views use GROUP BY to organize data by categories, showing sales data grouped by region, product category, or time period, with computed metrics for each group.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how aggregation views organize and compute metrics across different data groupings

diagrams.title: Querying Aggregation Views
diagrams.description: Shows the end-to-end process of how a user query against an aggregation view is processed, from the SQL query request through the view to the underlying base tables, and how the database optimizes this using materialization or on-the-fly computation.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how queries interact with aggregation views and the database's role in computing results
